entry.title: FSDreamTeam Updates GSX for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FSDreamTeam has released GSX 3.8.7, a large update for GSX, featuring simultaneous jetway/stairs boarding, Aviramp’s ramped staircase system, alongside a long list of changes, fixes, and backend improvements. The new Aviramp stairs are available in GSX Pro, and can be enabled through an airport profile. GSX now also offers a smarter gate selection menu, which hides stands that are too small for the current aircraft by default. This feature can be toggled on and off. As for simultaneous jetway and rear-stairs boarding/deboarding, when enabled in the airport customisation settings, GSX can now split passengers between the front jetway and the rear door. FSDreamTeam has also added a fallback system, so if a jetway fails to dock, boarding will switch to stairs automatically, instead of boarding no passengers at all. Moving on, there are improvements for airport customisation and handler scripts, expanded Python tools, better gate pre-selection options, and improved waypoint editing. The volume sliders now play a sound check when adjusted, making it easier to hear your changes immediately, and the handling for aircraft without GSX profiles has been added. There is also an expansion of the operator database, with several new handling and catering companies from around the world. ‍ Version 3.8.7 – March 31th, 2026 Full Release Notes ‍ GSX Pro can be purchased here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Updates 777 Lineup for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has recently issued an update to their Boeing 777 Lineup in Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a wealthy list of small changes and improvements, including a refactored C*U simulation. Build 2.04.138 represents a couple of months' worth of small fixes being implemented by their developers whenever they had free time during the development of the MSFS 2024 version of their 737 Lineup, with things speeding up once that came to a close. ​ The highlight of the update is a refactoring of the C*U fly-by-wire control system simulation, allowing their 777 to fly more like the real counterpart, retaining the normal pitch/speed interface pilots are used to, but also leveraging the fly-by-wire capabilities to enhance speed and pitch stability, making the flight more stable and pleasurable. ​ PMDG also makes sure to reinforce the fact that Boeing’s implementation of the fly-by-wire system is completely different from Airbus’ own approach, designed to function as an “ideal airplane” where their planes feel more like a regular non-FBW airplane, albeit on “steroids”. ​ Furthermore, they have also given attention to the datalink system, displays, certain cabin features, the flight management system, ground operations, hydraulic system, memory management, ground handling physics, pneumatics, radios, numerous other functions, and more. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ Aircraft Specific: The 777 lineup is available on PMDG’s website, with prices ranging from $59.99 to $77, depending on the variant at hand. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Releases Liverpool John Lennon Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently released their rendition of Liverpool John Lennon Airport (EGGP) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving Liverpool and the North West England/North Wales area, with an average of 5.6 million passengers per year. The airport began life in the early 1930s as Speke Airport, served by Imperial Airways with scheduled flights to Barton Aerodrome, Salford, Castle Bromwich Aerodrome, and Croydon Airport (near London). By the end of that first decade, the increasing demand for Irish Sea crossings led to the construction of a passenger terminal, a control tower, and two large aircraft hangars. Speke was requisitioned by the Royal Air Force during World War 2 to become RAF Speke, where a shadow factory was built to manufacture bombers. Lockheed Martin also used the plant to assemble fighters that were shipped to England from the United States. Normal civilian operations resumed immediately after the war ended, and passenger figures nearly doubled from 1945 to 1948, surpassing those at Manchester Airport (40 miles away). However, that leadership was short-lived because the airport was owned by the Ministry of Aviation, which greatly slowed its progress. The city only took back control of Liverpool Airport in 1961, rapidly preparing development plans to catch up with its rivals at Manchester. 5 years later, a new runway was opened, measuring 2,286 meters. A new passenger terminal was built in the 1980s to replace the 1930s terminal, which was repurposed as a hotel. In 1990, the airport was privatized, and British Aerospace took a 76% shareholding in the new company. It became a wholly owned subsidiary of the Peel Group. In 2000, work on a 42 million pound terminal began, tripling the airport’s size and passenger handling capabilities to ensure it could keep up with the ever-growing demand. ​ In 2005, a new apron was built exclusively for easyJet, one of the airport's main operators. In 2006, the main runway and taxiways were reconstructed for the first time since its opening in 1966. On top of the new pavement, they also added state-of-the-art CAT 3 ILS capabilities. ​ There are plans to expand the runway in the future to make it another hub for transatlantic/long-haul flights, and to construct a new cargo area south of the runway. It’s an operating base for easyJet, Jet2.com, and Ryanair. Aer Lingus, Loganair, Ryanair, and Wizz Air also serve it. The scenery features an accurate rendition of the airport, with hand-made ground textures, a true-to-life landside, various animated elements, custom night lighting, hand-crafted terminals, 180+ hand-crafted models, and much more. It’s available on the iniBuilds Store for roughly $15.81, requiring at least 5.59 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: A2A Simulations Publishes Roadmap Update for 2026 and Beyond
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. A2A Simulations has recently shared their roadmap for 2026 and 2027, announcing four new general aviation aircraft and a potential release window for their upcoming Legacy RG550, which was announced a few months ago. Given the timing (April 1st), many people were not quite sure whether the roadmap was true, but they have confirmed it is, in fact, real, and they will work their hardest to ensure these products come out within the specified window. After the release of the Accu-Sim Legacy RG550, they plan to bring over their famous Accu-Sim Cessna 172 trainer, one of their best-sellers during the Flight Simulator X/Prepar3D era, and highly regarded as one of the best general aviation airplanes ever conceived. It’s planned for the end of 2026. Next in line - now slotted for 2027 - is the Aerostar Turbo, although they are still evaluating the feasibility of its development at this point in time. Then, another two classics that were originally made for Flight Simulator X and Prepar3d, the Cessna 182 and the Piper Cherokee, both also slotted for 2027. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Black Square Updates Caravan Professional for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Black Square has released Version 1.1 of its Caravan Professional, the first big post-launch update to the aircraft, addressing everything from sound and systems to bug fixes. As for soundscape, Black Square has revised many areas of the engine sounds, including startup and lightoff, turbine spooling, beta range, and shutdown. Interior propeller noise has also been rebalanced, and the update has added new sounds for secondary injector activation, bleed air heating, the fuel pump, and engine starts with doors or windows open. For the Amphibian, v.1.1 has added the full package of MSFS 2024 water effects, including prop wash, float splashes, spray, bow waves, and water rudder trails. Black Square says it has also adjusted water depth behaviour, so the floats are now less likely to interact in weird ways with subsea terrain in rougher conditions. Moving on, the update includes a long list of changes to aircraft behaviour and systems. Among them are revised cabin temperature logic, a reduction in ITT, improved low and high idle behaviour, slightly earlier stall warning activation, and changes to the KAP 140’s default vertical navigation mode and annunciation. The generator will now also activate automatically after a successful start. Black Square has also addressed several issues affecting beta behaviour, engine sounds, autopilot rudder behaviour, water rudder operation, electrical logic, sound filtering on the ground, flap drag, emergency power lever fuel flow, and a range of smaller cockpit, tablet, and HUD issues. New Features: Bug Fixes: Full Release Notes V1.1 of the Caravan Professional is available to existing users from the “Your Orders” section of their Just Flight account. Purchase the aircraft for $47.99 USD through Just Flight. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FinnWest Aviation Releases EC135 Sound Enhancement Pack for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FinnWest Aviation has released a new sound replacement mod for the Nemeth Design EC135 (Local Legend 21) in MSFS 2024. Created by a real-world helicopter pilot, the add-on replaces the default sounds with a new, highly detailed sound set. According to FWX Aviation, the package includes reworked cockpit turbine sounds, a custom startup sequence, smoother torque transitions, and a full exterior sound replacement for replays, flybys, and external views. The Airbus H135, formerly the Eurocopter EC135, is one of the best-known light twin-engine helicopters in service. First flying in 1994 and entering service in 1996, it has become a familiar sight around the world in air ambulance, police, training, and utility roles, thanks to its low noise footprint, safety record, and operational flexibility. FWX Aviation is also offering a separate Rotor Haptic Engine, a standalone tool designed for bass shakers and transducers. The software reads live rotor RPM data through SimConnect, then generates physical rotor impulses for use with external hardware, such as ButtKicker Haptics. Full release notes ‍ Pricing starts at $9.20 USD for the sound pack, $4.59 USD for the haptic tool, and $12.66 USD for both together. You can buy them on the FWX website, and on SimMarket. The sound pack requires the Local Legend 21 Eurocopter EC135 to be downloaded in your sim. Interestingly, FWX says the haptic tool also works with other helicopters in the simulator, not just the EC135. You can find their Discord Server here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Flight Panels Releases X-Plane Profiles for Zibo 737 and C172
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Flight Panels has just launched its first products for X-Plane, following the company’s expansion announcement at Flight Sim Weekend 2026. The new range brings Stream Deck support to X-Plane 11 and 12 for the first time, with aircraft and autopilot profiles now available for the Zibo Boeing 737-800X and the Cessna 172. For the Zibo 737, Flight Panels says its aircraft profile includes 706 pre-programmed buttons across 31 pages, covering everything from overhead systems and communications to lighting and navigation. The Cessna 172 profile is obviously smaller, with 240 buttons across 11 pages, but follows the same idea. Autopilot profiles are also available for both aircraft, designed for the Stream Deck +, and making use of its physical buttons, touch screen, and rotary dials. According to Flight Panels, all of the new profiles are pre-configured and ready to use straight out of the box. Support is offered across the Stream Deck XL, MK.2, Stream Deck Mobile, Corsair Xeneon Edge, and, in the case of the autopilot products, the Stream Deck +. Flight Panels also used the launch to confirm that this is only the beginning of its X-Plane line-up, with the ToLiss Airbus A320neo expected to be the next release in April. The new products are available now through the Flight Panels store. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Pepelatz Announces Yakovlev Yak-18T for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Pepelatz has announced its next aircraft project for X-Plane, the Yakovlev Yak-18T, following the release of its Yak-55M last year. In a short development update on the X-Plane.Org forums, Pepelatz said work on the Yak-18T began immediately after the Yak-55M was done. The team added that the 3D model is now complete and that work is moving on to UV mapping. As expected at this stage, there is still no release window, with the developer saying it is simply focused on building the aircraft. Alongside the announcement, Pepelatz shared some preview screenshots, showing the aircraft already taking shape externally. Developed by Yakovlev, the Yak-18T is a four- or five-seat Soviet utility and aerobatic aircraft, originally introduced as a trainer for Aeroflot pilots, and powered by the Vedeneyev M14P radial engine. It is still early days for the project, and Pepelatz has not shared any timeline beyond the fact that development is underway. You can find the already-released Yak-55M here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Red Star Simulations Shares New MiG-17F Development Update for DCS
entry.summary: Sim: DCS. Red Star Simulations has published a new development report for its upcoming DCS: MiG-17F, focusing on the aircraft’s VK-1F engine and afterburner. The main takeaway is that the developer is aiming to recreate both the look and the handling cues of the MiG-17F’s afterburner in detail. According to Red Star, the module will model the distinctive exhaust flame and the slight delay before full afterburner thrust arrives, while also reflecting the fact that the real aircraft does not have a dedicated afterburner indicator light in the cockpit. Red Star says afterburner use in the MiG-17F will need to be handled with care, with the operator expected to watch engine temperatures and avoid overusing it. The developer also shared a rough idea of expected turning performance, noting that the MiG-17F should be capable of around 13 to 14 degrees per second at combat weight, rising to roughly 16 degrees per second with afterburner engaged. The MiG-17F remains in development for DCS, with no release date announced yet. Read the full post here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: MK Studios Teases Apr. 9 Release for Porto Airport
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has shared a few preview images for its upcoming Porto scenery for MSFS 2024, showing off more of the airport’s exterior, terminal interior, and lighting. Alongside the screenshots, the developer posted a short, slightly cryptic message, which at face value appears to point to an Apr. 9 release date. The new images show a wider look at the airport, including the runway, landside areas, apron scenes at night, and parts of the terminal interior. While this scenery is for MSFS 2024, MK Studios has said there will be a discounted upgrade path for users moving over from the 2020 version. View the full post here. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Announces Apr. 9 Release for 737-700 “High Detail Update”
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has previewed the finished “High Detail Update” for its 737 line in MSFS 2024, with the 737-700 set to be the first variant to receive it when it releases on Apr. 9. Alongside the announcement, PMDG also shared a new video showing before-and-after comparisons of the updated model up close. First announced in Nov. 2025, the High Detail Update brings much more detailed artwork to the 737, with reworked textures, improved geometry, and added detail across the exterior and interior. PMDG says the update includes “tens of thousands” of hand-placed rivets and fasteners, improved hatches and exterior controls, updated lighting, and improvements to the cockpit, cabin, cargo holds, landing gear, gear bays, and ground equipment. That is reflected in the new previews, which show everything from the flight deck and passenger cabin to the power connection, service areas, and gear bay. We saw a glimpse of this work earlier, through a LinkedIn post by artists at Gaya Studios, which worked with PMDG on the cabin interior. That new cabin is seemingly part of this “High Detail Update”. The 737-700 will launch first with the completed High Detail Update, while the 737-600, 737-800, and 737-900 will follow afterwards as updates. PMDG says the 737-600 is already in testing, with the others due to follow shortly after. These updated artwork updates will be integrated into the already released 737 for MSFS 2024, which comes with an upgrade price compared to the 2020 versions. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Pyreegue Dev Co. Previews Palma de Mallorca Airport for MSFS
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Pyreegue Dev Co. has further previewed Palma de Mallorca Airport (LEPA) in a new social media post, showcasing a handful of work-in-progress preview images. The project was first teased back in Oct. 2025. The screenshots show both exterior and interior areas of the airport, including parts of PMI Module C, with gates C38–C41 and C44–C48. As usual with Pyreegue, the level of detail looks to be quite high. While the post itself is very vague, the comment section quickly latched onto the shared pictures, with several users once again identifying the airport as Palma de Mallorca Airport. There is no release window yet, and Pyreegue has not shared any other platform details in the post. We’ll follow any upcoming developments closely. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Laminar Research Previews X-Plane 12.4.3 VR Update
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Laminar Research has shared an early look at X-Plane 12.4.3, which it is calling “The VR Update”, a significant rework of the simulator's Virtual Reality system. According to Laminar, work on the new VR system started last year, after the team began collecting feedback from Discord, social media, and the wider X-Plane community. The goal was to address some long-standing pain points in the current system. Laminar says the VR system has been rewritten from top to bottom, with a big focus on accessibility and ease of use. ‍ One of the main additions is support for the Apple Vision Pro, which Laminar says will utilise Nvidia's CloudXR. This does not include a native version of X-Plane running on the headset itself, but rather the sim running on a PC and streaming to the device. That also means you will need a PC with an Nvidia GPU. It will not run from a MacBook. When support does arrive, those wanting to use it will need to download a free companion app from the VisionOS store. ‍ Mixed reality support is also on the way for compliant devices with passthrough, letting the user combine X-Plane’s outside view with their real-world cockpit hardware. Using ARKit-based masking tools for the Vision Pro, or similar QR-based spatial tracking for other headsets, you will also be able to mask out frames from the real-life-world, and import them into your sim environment as a projection. In turn, this allows you to project your physical hardware panels onto your virtual cockpit, so you can interact with it in VR. Hand tracking is also being added, along with updated cockpit manipulation for both hands and traditional controllers. ‍ Laminar also says 12.4.3 will support foveated streaming through CloudXR for the Apple Vision Pro. OpenXR users will instead use something called Variable Rate Super Sampling, or VRSS, on supported headsets. Both of these technologies do much the same thing. X-Plane will keep the part of the image you are actually looking at in high detail, while reducing detail in your peripheral vision to save performance. Moving on, VR is getting a new interface, new gesture controls, and a wrist-based radial menu on some devices. As for compatibility, mixed reality should work on compliant OpenXR headsets with passthrough, while hand tracking and eye tracking will depend on headset support. Laminar says that, due to the Meta Quest 3 not allowing these features for PC use, it will sadly not be supported. They add that 12.4.3 will include some non-VR content as well, but that will be shown later, after 12.4.2 has been released. ‍ Take a look at the full blog post here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Just Flight Releases Cessna 152 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Just Flight has recently released the Cessna 152 for X-Plane 12, developed in partnership with Thranda Design. After bringing its PA-28 fleet over, Just Flight has now given the same treatment to the popular two-seat trainer, with a new XP12-native version. The aircraft is based on the real-world airframe G-BGAE, based at Conington Airfield, and comes with six paint schemes covering operators from the UK, the United States, Switzerland, Italy, Germany, and Czechia. Thranda’s Dynamic Livery system is included too, letting users create their own paint schemes from within the EFB. The C152 has PBR materials, real-time reflections, 8K textures, HDR lighting, native X-Plane 12 rain effects, and a long list of animations, including doors, windows, vents, sun visors, and even an ignition keychain that moves with G-forces. Inside the cockpit, Just Flight has modelled a full avionics suite, VR support, custom engine and electrical logic, interactive checklists and a feature-rich EFB with weight and balance tools, aircraft options, engine condition pages, and the Thranda livery controls. Just Flight also highlights a custom-coded electrical system with working circuit breakers, vapour lock and spark plug fouling simulation, custom sounds, and updated flight dynamics built around X-Plane 12’s newer flight model. ‍ MODEL COCKPIT AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS LIVERIESThe C152 is supplied in the following six paint schemes: OTHER FEATURES If you already own the X-Plane 11 version, Just Flight says a 25% discount is available. The aircraft costs $42.99 USD for new customers, and you can purchase it here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024 to v1.0.1
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has released the first update for their newly released L1011 TriStar Airliner, version 1.0.1. It is a mostly quality-of-life update, refining many aspects of the new release and improving stability and user experience. On the systems side, iniBuilds has worked through a long list of fixes across the INS, annunciators, gear lever behaviour, VNAV and PMS performance, ILS and flight director logic, cabin temperature, pressurisation, anti-ice indications, spoiler behaviour, ground spoilers, autobrake simulation, auto throttle disconnect logic, and more. Now included are animated circuit breakers, tied into the electrical system, plus tooltips for them. As for artwork, iniBuilds has fixed the floating wipers issue, dark cockpit window tint, displaced weathering, wing z-fighting, incorrect jetbridge behaviour on non-lounge variants, and a hole near the APU exhaust. Some liveries have also been corrected, including placards, logos, and cabin decals, and, in one of the more random additions in the changelog, lavatory doors are now operable. Brightness on the take-off card is now tied to EFB priority selection, auto-complete on start now matches the calculated flap setting more closely, and overall night brightness has been reduced. iniBuilds has also added the ability to switch between take-off and landing cards by pressing the top of the card. As for soundscape, there is a fix targeting incorrect V1 and rotate call behaviour. ‍ V1.0.1SYSTEMS ⚙️FIXED INS MSU not detented to/from NAV positionFIXED Light test switch doesn't fully illuminate all annunciators (especially on Main Instrument Panel)FIXED Cabin climb indicator makes occasional leaps up & down when sitting still on the groundFIXED Gear lever desyncs when using peripheralsFIXED TM not respecting PMS constraints at calculated valueFIXED Speed vertical modes need to command pitch much more readilyFIXED ILS mode remains on with AP/FD offFIXED FD ILS LOC guidance unsatisfactoryFIXED Very cold cabin temperatureFIXED PMS VNAV control is not fast or accurate enough leading to consistent speed under/overshootsFIXED Cabin lights remain on with no elec. powerFIXED Engine 1 & 3 anti ice ON not illuminating HEATFIXED Pressurization PSI needle animation issueFIXED TOGA flag still missing from AFCS mode annunciatorFIXED Incorrect ATS DISC flag when using TMFIXED Spoiler lever retracts automatically after landingFIXED Ground spoilers not extending fully on reverse selectionFIXED Cabin heat not increasing past indicated limitFIXED Autobrake simulationFIXED Fire detection loop test knobs & Fire bell sound.FIXED Nacelle/pylon ovht detection test knobs & emissive.FIXED Engine turbine cooling air panel test emissive.FIXED Wheel well fire test panelFIXED Humidity control panel button animation.FIXED Forward cargo compartment logic for guarded switches with labels.FIXED N2 test buttons & logicFIXED Engine oil, temp & press test buttons & logicFIXED #3 Engine TGT & all Celsius Gauges INOPFIXED Rudder Axis binding issue for gamepad controllerFIXED FMA test doesn't work on F/O sideFIXED Fire Bell cutout requires subsequent press to silence alarmFIXED Walkaround - ACU disconnects immediately after clicking on air ventFIXED Auto throttle not disconnecting when weight on wheels has above nil valueFIXED SPOILER ON/OFF control commands not workingADDED Circuit breaker animation and wired to basic electrical system functions.ADDED Tooltips to all CB. ART 🎨FIXED Wipers are floating above windshieldFIXED Standby altimeter animation errorFIXED ADF freq has no integral lightFIXED Transponder digits are brighter than comm radiosFIXED Excessive weathering effectFIXED Jetbridge attempts to connect to lounge even in non-lounge variantsFIXED Door profile incomplete? See referenceFIXED Wipers do not wipe away moisture on windshieldFIXED Covert sunshades to draggable elementFIXED Mechanic collision with ENG1 doorsFIXED Wipers in any LOD other than 0 are in the old parked positionFIXED Hole in rear of fuselage near APU exhaustFIXED Cockpit window tint too darkFIXED Cargo doors weathering displaced, floating above modelFIXED Aircraft Weathering not parented to door animationsFIXED Weathering elements on tail floating above fuselageFIXED z-fighting on wings with weathering enabledFIXED Cathay VR-HHX - Door decals in cabinFIXED Novair - Logo mirrored and font accuracyFIXED All Liveries - Cockpit Placard Shows G-STAR / AB-CD Instead of Actual Reg/SelcalADDED Operable lavatory doors PFB📜FIXED Tie Takeoff card brightness to selected EFB as decided by priority switchFIXED Auto-Complete on start doesn't match flaps from calculated valueFIXED Reduced overall brightness at nightADDED Can now switch between T/O & Land cards with top of card press SOUNDS🔊FIXED v1 & rotate calls when play when decelerating to bugged speed The update is available now through the MSFS 2024 in-sim Marketplace. It can be purchased for $49.99 USD. The aircraft is not yet out on the iniBuilds Store or other vendors. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Parallel 42 Releases ChasePlane SU5 Beta Build for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Parallel 42 has released the first experimental ChasePlane build, now with Sim Update 5 beta support for MSFS 2024. The news was shared in a blog post on Parallel 42's website. According to the team, the last six months have been spent on refining the camera add-on, fixing various issues, and working around sim limitations. Among the biggest changes is the incorporation of Asobo’s new Camera API. Parallel 42 says it began reworking ChasePlane shortly after Asobo announced the new camera tools for Sim Update 5, and this beta build already uses the new system where the older code was no longer enough. The move also puts ChasePlane onto the framework it will need going forward, with the new Camera API giving Parallel 42 a much clearer path towards VR support in MSFS 2024, something that has been harder to achieve than in the MSFS 2020 version. Parallel 42 says work on VR for MSFS 2024 will begin once Sim Update 5 leaves beta. Sim Update 5 support comes with the downside of the copilot avatar no longer fading away when the camera gets too close, which means some view presets may clip into the model. You can fix this by nudging the view forward or removing the copilot entirely in the settings. The issue has already been reported to Asobo, and the team expects it to be addressed in a later Sim Update 5 beta build or early in the Sim Update 6 cycle. With SU5, toolbar add-ons, such as ChasePlane, now work in Career Mode as well, which removes one of the biggest limitations the add-on has had in MSFS 2024 since launch. If you want to try the new experimental build, download the latest ChasePlane Manager from your store account. The addon is available for $30.00 USD on the P42 store. The known limitations page has also been updated with the new release. Keep in mind that this is an experimental release, and that things may break again with future beta releases from Microsoft. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
